Driven into a corner by the invading mobile suits of the Principality of Zeon, the Federation Forces developed this melee combat MS based on the development plan known as Operation V, which was intended to turn the tide of the war.
Because it was designed for combat data collection and versatile functionality, it has a Core Block system compatible with the Guncannon and Guntank, Luna Titanium alloy armor, a beam rifle with the power of a warship's main gun, and other technologies that were cutting edge at the time of its creation.
It was among the best of the mobile weapons used during the One Year War.
It was so versatile that it could fight everywhere from space to undersea with almost no modifications, and was even able to enter the atmosphere on its own.
The Gundam and its superior performance continued to pile up victories as the main weapon of the White Base Corps, and it was widely feared by the Zeon Forces, who nicknamed it the "Federation's White Devil."

This customized machine is a variation of the Principality of Zeon's Zaku II, based on the Zaku II F and remodeled for use by commanders and veteran pilots.
It is painted red to match the personal colors of Char Aznable, an ace also known as the "Red Comet."
Its key features include a head-mounted blade antenna to signify it as a commander type, 30% higher engine output, and increased propellant.
These additions do little to improve the machine's performance, however, and the rumors that it moves an amazing three times faster than normal are largely a result of the skill of its pilot.
It can use a variety of armaments depending on the situation, allowing it to adapt to different types of battle.
In addition to its versatility, extensibility, and ease of production, it also has excellent handling and reliability.

A prototype mobile suit created by Zimmad, in hopes that it would be formally adopted as the mainline MS of the Principality of Zeon.
It was originally developed as the EMS-04 Zudah, a high-performance machine with excellent mobility, which employed a Saturn engine that could run on extremely heavy propellants such as lead, zinc, and bismuth.
Together with the AMBAC system, this high thrust and acceleration enabled it to make extremely rapid turns, which ironically exceeded the machine's limits and caused it to break up in mid-air during an official test flight.
This disaster led to the EMS-04's official rejection.
However, it underwent structural improvements and was resurrected as the supposedly all-new EMS-10 Zudah, a high-performance machine far surpassing the Zaku.
Tragically, the defects of the original design could not be resolved, and it is not even certain whether the planned upgrade work was actually carried out.
Four prototype units were stationed aboard the test support ship Jotunheim for assessment by the 603rd Technical Evaluation Unit.
The ill-fated machine broke apart yet again, ending all chances of mass production for good.
This is the first unit, assigned to Lt.
Commander Jean Luc Duvall.
The horn on its head indicates that it is a commander machine.

This one-off remodel was created using various parts, including some from a GM Ground Type, to repair a Gundam Ground Type that was heavily damaged during an attempt to capture the Principality of Zeon's Apsaras II.
The Gundam Ground Type was experimentally produced in small numbers during the early stages of the Federation Forces' MS mass production plan.
Because much of its armor and internal parts were recycled or reproduced from surplus Gundam parts, it lacked a stock of replacement parts, and so bases often had to make field repairs using parts from other machines and equipment.
When Shiro Amada's Gundam Ground Type was incapacitated in the battle against the Apsaras II, his ideas were combined with the combat data collected up to that point to perform a complete overhaul, in the hope of improving the original machine's ground combat performance.
Ez8, or "Extra-Zero-Eight," is not an official model number.
This refers to the "81" on the machine's chest armor, which represents the first unit of the 08th MS Team, or in other words the commander's machine.

A high-performance Gundam-type mobile suit, developed at the Augusta base on Earth as part of the Federation Forces' next-generation development plan known as the G-4 Project.
Its drive and control systems incorporate the very latest technology for use by Newtypes.
The entire drive system uses magnet coating technology, and a panoramic monitor provides a wider field of view during visual-range combat.
It also features a linear seat that reduces damage from impact and vibration, reducing the strain on the pilot.
Originally, this machine was slated for assignment to the 13th Independent Unit, rumored to be a Newtype corps.
However, it was attacked by a Zeon special forces team during its final testing, resulting in its partial destruction and preventing its deployment.

A machine that the Earth Federation Force created specifically to test the EXAM system that a Zeon Newtype research facility developed. It is built based on a Gundam Ground Type but has since been modified and enhanced.
The unit is designed with compatibility with the EXAM system in mind, around the concept of solo assaults and high-mobility combat. It is equipped with a variety of mobility-enhancing features, multiple built-in weapons, and has undergone extensive tuning for it to excel in close quarters.
Only the unit number 1 has the GM-type head from initial testing installed on it.

A federation forces Gundam-type mobile suit assigned to the moore brotherhood, an organization made up of former redidents of the destroyed side 4.
Its design differs from the FA-78-1 version and includes an array of experimental systems and equipment.
This machine is equipped with extraordinarily heavy armament compared to other MS of the time.
Its right arm bears a large twin beam rifle which employs an energy pack system.
In addition to a shield, the left arm carries five rocket launchers.
Furthermore, the backpack holds missile pods and a large beam cannon, as well as six spare energy packs, and its sub-arms allow the machine to carry a total of four shields.
Thrusters and apogee motors are attached to the shoulders, legs, and elsewhere.
Combined with the backpack's large rocket boosters, these give it thrust rivaling that of a high mobility type MS.

An experimental mobile suit, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
This successor to the Gundam concept that had proved so successful during the One Year War was equipped with a learning computer, a Core Block system, and other technologies.
Its performance was almost 30% better than the Gundam's, but because its environmental adaptation was somewhat lacking, it was designed with the ability to switch out its main parts for a Core Fighter II.
This allowed it to operate both on land and in space.

An experimental mobile suit, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
This heavy assault machine was developed as an MS with the ultimate in offensive strength, which could annihilate enemies and their bases in any environment or situation.
Its most distinctive feature is a nuclear weapon known as the Atomic Bazooka.
To withstand the blasts of its own attacks, all its armor has anti-heat and anti-impact features, and it must carry a special shield with an internal cooling system.
Each shoulder is equipped with high-output thrusters to aid in attacks and escapes, and the machine possesses better reaction speed and mobility than its appearance would imply.
It was to have had its tactical nuclear strike capabilities tested at the Torrington base, but was seized and lost when the Delaz Fleet attacked the base.

A high-performance transformable mobile suit developed as part of Project Zeta, which was spearheaded by the Anti Earth Union Group (AEUG).
Its lightweight structure is extremely sturdy, thanks to the new alloy known as Gundarium Gamma.
It is powered by advanced ultra-compact fusion reactors, and its transformation mechanisms use movable frame technology appropriated from the Titans.
The machine can change from MS form into a flight form known as a "waverider," allowing it to enter the atmosphere with only its standard equipment.
It retains its attack capabilities even while descending, and can shift immediately into combat operations under gravity, a truly revolutionary feature.
Its ranged weapons include a beam rifle that can also function as a long beam saber for close combat, and a hyper mega launcher, the most powerful MS weapon of its era.
This gives the Zeta Gundam great offensive strength as well as high versatility.

A handmade prototype mobile suit developed aboard the Jupitris, a resource transportation ship that traveled between Jupiter and Earth, for the personal use of its designer Paptimus Scirocco.
This was Scirocco's fourth machine after the Messala, Palace Athene, and Bolinoak Sammahn.
Though its weapons are fairly standard, it is armored with a huge mass of Gundarium alloy, and its body unit houses a generator whose output surpasses that of an entire MS.
Its front skirt armor contains "hidden arms" that can be used for close combat and melee attacks, and the 50 thrusters all over its body can adapt to any vector.
For such a large machine, it has surprising mobility and acceleration, and its unique technology gives this machine extraordinary specs.
Although it is equipped with a psycommu system, what Zeon and the Earth Federation call "Newtype ability" is defined as "psychic" in the Jupiter Sphere, and so their devices use a different approach from the Federation and Anaheim Electronics.

MA prototype transformable mobile suit developed by the AEUG's Project Zeta.
For the sake of maximum offensive power and versatility, this machine was designed to combine the functions of the One Year War-era Gundam and G-Mecha.
This MS specializes in high output and high firepower, as per the trend at its creation, and is thus technologically state-of-the-art.
It is armed with a high mega cannon and various other powerful weapons, but its operating time with default equipment at maximum power is very short, and some claim that its endurance is less than 15 minutes.
Another feature is its transformation into the G-Fortress, a mobile armor form with 1.5 times the thrust of its MS form, giving it the utmost mobility.
It can also separate into a Core Top made up of its upper body and a Core Block, a Core Base made up of its lower body and a Core Block, and a Core Fighter that forms its abdomen and main cockpit.
These three sections also function as aerospace fighter craft.

Developed by Axis with the aim of turning the Newtype-use Mobile Armor Elmeth—pride of the One Year War—into a mobile suit.
The biggest hurdle to overcome was reducing the size and weight of the unmanned portable gun battery "bit," which was achieved by adopting the energy CAP method and making it possible to refill energy and fuel from the mobile suit itself.
The ability to carry psycommu weapon "Funnels" for all-range attacks was also implemented.
Its unique fuselage, with a terminal built into its rearward-extending head for transmitting and receiving psycho-waves, allows it to functionally double as a Newtype Machine.
By retooling the propulsion unit into a four-piece shoulder binder, its angle can be adjusted to achieve response and mobility rates that far outpace those of other mobile suits of the era in times of high mobility warfare.
Piloted by Haman Karn since the end of the Gryps War, her unparalleled piloting skill has exhibited such masterful and exemplary combat power that it has cemented a strong image of Axis among its contemporaries.

A prototype mobile suit for Newtypes, personally designed by Amuro Ray of One Year War fame.
This machine was designed based on generations of Gundam-type data and equipped with the best technology available at the time.
It has incredible responsiveness due to its partial use of psycho-frame, a revolutionary material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality.
On its back are fin funnels, special Newtype weapons controlled through psycommu to unleash all-range attacks.
Combining three or more fin funnels allows them to be used as a beam weapon-nullifying I-Field generator, and thus these high-performance weapons combine both attack and defense.

Developed for the personal use of Char Aznable, the leader of the revived Neo Zeon, this prototype mobile suit for Newtypes was the culmination of all Zeon mobile suit research and development.
It was therefore a true symbol of the reborn Neo Zeon forces.
Because it was developed to be an outstanding MS, as well as a full-spec Newtype machine that could perform all-range attacks with remote weapons, it was heavily armored and packed with thrusters and apogee motors to give it high mobility.
The machine achieved incredible responsiveness thanks to its partial use of psycho-frame, a material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality.
Its fixed armament included a diffuse mega particle cannon, and with a total of six funnels on its back, it was a high-performance machine that was incredibly balanced.

The prototype Unicorn Gundam, secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, features a system called NT-D which expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.

The prototype Banshee Norn, secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, features a system called NT-D which expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.
The treasure of the Banshee Norn is its Armed Armor XC, which not only relaxes the conditions for the activation of Destroy Mode, but also maintains a heightened affinity between pilot and psycommu after the activation.
The expansion mechanisms of each body part work in the same fashion as the extra psycho-frame armaments added to the Banshee Norn, meaning that the lion-maned Armed Armor XC transforms the Banshee into an absolute beast.

A fifth-generation mobile suit developed by Anaheim Electronics was prepared for Mafty in order to raid the Adelaide Conference.
It is a sibling unit to the Penelope but incorporates even more advanced technology due to its built-in Minovsky Flight Unit.
Its total height surpasses 26 meters and its arms are especially more massive when compared to standard mobile suits, resulting in a monstrous silhouette despite being named a Gundam.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo Japan.
As the Mobile Fighter developed for the 13th Gundam Fight, it was designed by Dr.Mikamura.
Notable for being able to switch combat modes in response to its pilot's emotions, it can also utilize its special move, the Shining Finger, upon reaching its second stage, Battle Mode.
In its third stage, Super Mode, all of its cooling covers fully open and the entire machine shines golden.
In this state, its pilot can unleash its full fighting potential.

A Mobile Fighter used by Master Asia, Undefeated of the East in the 13th Gundam Fight.
How this unit was developed remains a mystery.
One theory says that Master Asia used the Shuffle Heart he piloted during his time in the Shuffle Alliance and completed this unit with the combination of the DG (Dark Gundam) Cells.
The structure of the unit is able to effectively reproduce Master Asia's combat ability, generating multiple ultimate moves of The School of Master Asia.
Since DG Cells cannot erode organisms with a strong mentality, Master Asia is able to overcome the DG Cells with his robust spirit, and repair the unit using the DG Cells without being taken over by it.

A Mobile Fighter developed by Neo Japan for the 13th Gundam Final Fight.
This unit has all 11-months of tournament data of the Shining Gundam embedded into it, and its performance under normal condition matches up to the Shining Gundam's super mode.
Various parts and manipulators have been modified and improved, as well as the linkage with the pilot, Domon Kasshu, to achieve a higher accuracy of reflecting Domon's combat ability and style.
One of the greatest features, Hyper Mode, is activated by using the energy multiplier that amplifies the pilot's spirits and allows them to concentrate their energy.
When activated, the unit can perform its final move, Erupting Burning Finger, and The School of Master Asia's ultimate move, Sekiha Tenkyoken.

A prototype unit created by the five Gundam Scientists 15 years prior to the start of Operation Meteor.
Created to be the supreme Gundam, it was developed with a razor-sharp focus on performance with little thought given to ease of production or practicality.
Wielding Twin Buster Rifles powerful enough to destroy whole colonies and a cockpit equipped with a performance- enhancing Zero System, it is peerless in terms of sheer combat ability.
Development was halted due to its overwhelming destructive power and the instability of the Zero System, but it was put into action after Quatre Raberba Winner obtained the schematics and completed the suit himself.

This mobile suit was developed at the behest of OZ leader Treize Khushrenada and based on the Tallgeese and five Gundams.
Its ability to transform allows it to fly at high speeds for extended periods of time.
In keeping with Treize's wishes, it does away with projectile weapons in favor of a Beam Sword and Heat Rod, granting it unmatched performance in close quarters.
A Zero System similar to the Wing Gundam Zero's grants it high performance along with a risk of driving the pilot completely insane.
The suit passed from the hands of Treize to Heero Yuy before being piloted by Milliardo Peacecraft.

A high-stakes weapon developed by the United Nations Earth as a trump card to counter the Space Revolutionary Army's colony drops during the 7th Space War.
Possessing a Flash System to maximize Newtype pilots' combat abilities, it is able to control unmanned drones called Bit Mobile Suits.
It is also equipped with a Satellite System, allowing its energy supply to be transmitted directly from the Super Microwave facility D.O.M.E., located on the surface of the moon.
This energy supply also powers the Gundam X's greatest weapon, the large Satellite Cannon, which possesses the power to destroy even a colony with a single shot.
However, in order to receive energy via Super Microwave, a Newtype was required to access the SMW facility upon the system's first use.
In addition, this unit cannot be started up without a control device called G-Controller.
A unit known for its power, although difficult to operate.

A Mobile Suit developed by the New United Nations Earth after the war using the latest technology based on Gundam X.
Since it inherits the concept and data of Gundam X, and the cockpit has been installed as it is.
The Satellite System can be started up without a Newtype.
In addition, the Satellite Cannon is upgraded to the Satellite System Mk-II, a two-barrelled Twin Satellite Cannon armed with overwhelming attack power.
With this improvement, the unit can fire multiple shots with the Twin Satellite Cannons with only one microwave energy absorption.
Other armaments have also been comprehensively strengthened to meet the performance level required for the strongest Mobile Suit in After War.
This unit also requires a specialized G-Controller to start up, but it can also be activated using the GX-9900 G-Controller.

A successor to Gundam Virsago developed by the Government Reconstruction Committee to fight against the Space Revolutionary Army.
The Government Reconstruction Committee, established among the upper class of the former United Nations Earth, develops the unit with the intention to resurrect Gundam, a high-takes weapon that is said to be the symbol of the United Nations Earth in the previous Great War.
One of them is the Gundam Virsago.
This unit inherits its characteristics, and is able to adapt to the space environment together with improved overall combat ability.
Initially, the Gundam Virsago, which had a high overall ability is redeveloped to further specialize in its artillery combat ability.
Among them, the Mega Sonic Cannon, a powerful armament is added to the chest and two additions to the abdomen.
Also, the output has been enhanced and implemented as Triple Mega Sonic Cannon.
It is capable of doing a link-up with the Gundam Ashtaron Hermit Crab, where the unit receives microwave energy and transfers it to the consort unit to release a powerful attack, Satellite Launcher, which has the same potential as the Satellite Cannon.

A ZGMF-X series suit, developed based on data from stolen Alliance GAT-X series units and created as a joint project between ZAFT's Mobile Suit divisions.
With the devastating output provided by its Neutron Jammer Canceller nuclear reactor and virtually unlimited use of Phase Shift Armor, its performance far outstrips that of the GAT-X series.
Intended to hold its own against entire crowds of Mobile  Suits, its devastating arsenal includes Balaena Plasma Beam Cannons in each wing, Xiphias Railguns on each hip, and a multi-lock-on system with the ability to track multiple targets at once.
The sheer amount of offensive might is enough to spell instant doom for an entire fleet.
Deploying the wings on its back will shift the suit into HiMAT (High Mobility Aerial Tactics) mode, granting it superior maneuverability whether on- or off-planet.

A ZGMF-X series suit, developed based on data from stolen Alliance GAT-X series units and created as a joint project between ZAFT's Mobile Suit divisions.
With the devastating output provided by its Neutron Jammer Canceller nuclear reactor and virtually unlimited use of Phase Shift Armor, its performance far outstrips that of the GAT-X series.
The suit's most distinguishing characteristic by far is the Fatum-00 unit mounted on its back.
Though it can give an added boost compared to traditional propulsion units, it can also be detached for use in remote attacks or as a sub-flight system.
Armed with twin Lacerta Beam Sabers which can be combined into a single blade, as well as Bassel Beam Boomerangs concealed in both shoulders, this machine is capable of unleashing unconventional attacks when linked with a decoupled Fatum-00.

One of the Gundam Astray series of prototype Mobile Suits developed by Morgenroete Inc.(an Orb Company) utilizing misappropriated GAT-X series data.
They never adopted Phase Shift Armor, but by utilizing a foam metal for the armor and cutting back considerably on armor bulk, the Red Frame came out much lighter, giving it an incredible level of agility.
It also enabled the use of Beam Weapons that never made it to full adoption by ZAFT Mobile Suits at the time.
This is the second of the Gundam Astrays, intended to be a testbed for ways to allow Naturals to pilot Mobile Suits.
It utilizes an unfinished prototype OS.
It was built in the neutral colony Heliopolis, but when the colony was destroyed in a battle between ZAFT's Creuset Team and the Earth Alliance's forces, it was recovered by Lowe Guele of the Junk Guild, who made it his own personal machine from that point forward.
Due to the heavy energy consumption of Beam Sabers, it is equipped with a gigantic suit-sized katana called the Gerbera Straight, which it uses as its primary armament.

The Dreadnought Gundam, with its extra module affixed.
This is how the Dreadnought Gundam looks with four newly- developed large DRAGOON units on its back.
The DRAGOON units were produced and affixed at Ame-no- Mihashira by Lowe Guele, based on design docs furnished by Andrew Waldfeld.
Each DRAGOON is connected to the main unit via cables.
Feeding energy through these cables is what enables the use of Phase Shift Armor.
It can also deploy beams between each turret, creating a defensive field.
The machine's name is one that Lowe gave it.
The "X" comes from the shape of the DRAGOON units; and the "Astray" comes from the fact that it is a weapon, yet is intended to be used peacefully, ergo it strays from its fundamental purpose.
Prayer Reverie piloted this machine and put its impressive might to full use in battle against the Super Hyperion Gundam stolen from the Eurasian Federation.

A mobile suit rebuilt from the GAT-X105 Strike that survived the last great war using spare parts that belonged to the Rogue that were provided by Cagalli.
When Phase Shift is activated, the color of its armor conforms to the Alliance's Strike.
On the other hand, its Twin Eye is green because it incorporates spare parts from the Rouge.
It is designed to perform like the first-generation GAT-X105 Strike, and some of its components cannot keep up with Kira's abilities as an ace pilot.
Nevertheless, by replacing worn and deteriorated parts with new ones, it maintains a high level of performance at the limit of its design in terms of responsiveness and durability.
The DRAGOON Striker was completed at Orb and the Factory using design data from a prototype Striker Pack for DRAGOON operation planned by the Alliance.
Kira, having learned of the DRAGOON System's existence when he engaged the Providence, wanted to incorporate it on a trial basis.
The Dragoon Striker evolved from the AQM/E-X04 Gun Barrel Striker, which operated four Wired Gunbarrels, and comes equipped with three completely separate DRAGOONs.
During the design stage, it became clear that the Strike's generator did not produce sufficient power output.
However, by connecting multiple battery packs—the same used in the Perfect Strike—a bespoke solution was finally arrived at.
While the DRAGOON Striker had issues with the energy efficiency of its generator, its firepower was ideal.
After Kira lost the Freedom, he used the DRAGOON Striker to respond to emergencies, which proved useful in providing him with combat experience in a mobile suit equipped with the DRAGOON System.
It is possible that Kira's experience with the DRAGOON Striker was one reason he was able to adapt so quickly to the DRAGOON System in the Strike Freedom.

The original doctrine of the Orb Forces was to maintain a nonaggressive defense, but after suffering defeat at the hands of the Earth Alliance Forces when they invaded, reconsidering that doctrine became an imperative, and the Orb Forces began to adopt a proactive defense, preemptively striking the enemy before they approach the homeland.
As a result, aerial carriers capable of conducting foreign campaigns became a necessity, and the Orb Forces began to explore developing carrier-based machines as its primary mobile armor.
In the process, several designs were considered and prototyped in parallel, and this machine was one of the test models of the Murasame developed with mass production in mind, as well as one of the few machines actually built for testing from among the many considered.
As a prototype, some of its parts, such as its wings, are replaceable, enabling a variety of tests to be conducted with a single machine.
The version lent to Athrun, whom Lacus entrusted to handle the remnants of the Zala faction who descended to Earth, was selected after taking the nature of the mission into account based on the suitability of its parts for long operational time and long-distance flight.
Additionally, while its performance is extremely high, it is nevertheless too peaky, and only an ace pilot can draw out its true potential.
Consequently, these tests parts were not adopted in the mass production version of the Murasame.
Still, they succeeded in collecting valuable operational data in combat, which in turn was used to complete the mass production version.

One of the Impulse Gundam's equipment variants.
Attaching the Force Silhouette pack gives the Impulse high mid-range combat mobility.
With the addition of powerful thrusters and wings, the Impulse is now capable of flight.
The top of the backpack also includes two Vajra Beam Sabers developed for the Second Stage Series.

One of the Second Stage Series machines developed at the request of PLANT Supreme Council Chairman Gilbert Dullindal.
Boasting supremely high performance courtesy of the hybrid-nuclear Hyper Deuterion System, it possesses even higher performance than other Second Stage Series suits.
It's equipped with a variety of armaments originally belonging to the Impulse Gundam's Silhouette System, giving it all-around performance to handle any situation.
In addition to powerful weaponry like its Palma Fiocina Palm Beam Cannon and High-Energy Long-Range Beam Cannon, the Destiny can also deploy the wings on its back for high-mobility atmospheric flight.
The Destiny also excels defensively, with an expandable Anti-Beam Shield, and a Solidus Fulgor beam shield generator.

A general-purpose ZAFT Mobile Suit developed as the successor to the Providence Gundam.
Boasting supremely high performance courtesy of the hybrid-nuclear Hyper Deuterion System, it possesses even higher performance than other Second Stage Series suits.
It carries forward the Providence Gundam's DRAGOON System, with a more powerful Beam Cannon and the ability to have the two large DRAGOONs form spike-like beam blades to penetrate enemy defenses.
The Legend is also installed with a next-generation control system that even pilots lacking finely-honed spatial awareness skills can handle.
With defensive capabilities that include the Solidus Fulgor beam shield generators on its hands, the Legend has a staggeringly high level of overall performance.

A Celestial Being 3rd-generation Gundam, developed for actual combat based on the 2nd-gen Gundams' test data.
With a solar-powered GN Drive to provide virtually limitless amounts of energy and heretofore unseen handheld Beam Weapons, its performance far exceeds any current Mobile Suit.
It is also equipped with a Trans-Am System, which releases stored, highly-compressed particles from every surface to boost its output to nearly three times its specifications for a short time.
The GN Particles generated by the Drive make the Exia an astoundingly agile close-combat machine even among other Gundams of its generation.
Specialized for close fights, its weaponry includes four Beam Sabers and three swords, a combination which gave it the development code "Seven Swords."
The swords' ability to cut through a defensive GN Shield makes it evident that this model was made to fight Gundams.

A Mobile Suit developed by Flit Asuno in cooperation with the Earth Federation Forces to fight against the Unknown Enemy (UE).
This Gundam was developed by incorporating Flit's theory based on the data from the AGE Device, a device entrusted to him by his mother, Marina Asuno.
One of the biggest features is that the AGE System, installed in the center of the unit, evolves the Mobile Suit by using the living organism mechanism and evolution database.
It is possible to create new Mobile Suit weapons and armaments using the accumulated battle data by linking up to the unit production and analyzer, AGE Builder.
In addition, the power source is a plasma compression furnace similar to the UE, which also uses higher grade armor, allowing its performance to be better than the UE's Mobile Suit.

A Mobile Suit developed by the Mobile Suit Technology Research and Development Department of the Earth Federation Forces under the instruction of Flit Asuno, who was the developer of the AGE System and a Gundam pilot.
 Developed as an advanced model of the Gundam AGE-1, this unit transforms into Strider Form that enables high-speed movement, and the AGE System produces data to ensure its superiority on the battlefield.
In this mode, the acceleration speed is three times that of normal speed.
 Also, the Hyper DODS Rifle equipped has twice the power of the conventional armament.
It has the firepower that can overpower the opponent's combat ability.
  Gundam AGE-2 is well-known for its AGE System and later a piece of specialized equipment, Double Bullet was created.
A wear that enhances the power of normal and beam weapons.

An improved form of the Gundam Barbatos.
This form is close to the suit's original form from the time of the Calamity War, and it was reproduced by Saisei engineers based on ancient materials recorded in Teiwaz's database.
Although its performance has not been restored to the same level as during the Calamity War, the frame's weight balance and reactors have been readjusted to improve its overall capabilities.
In addition, the Alaya-Vijnana System has been adjusted for use by its pilot, Mikazuki Augus.
It has also been equipped with a new Long Sword made of a rare alloy and is suitable for making targeted attacks.

An improved form of the Gundam Barbatos Lupus.
This suit is the result of a major upgrade to the Barbatos Lupus at Saisei's Mobile Suit workshop after it was severely damaged during its desperate battle against the Mobile Armor Hashmal.
In line with the combat data of Mikazuki Augus, who increased his synchronization with the suit through the Alaya-Vijnana System, its reaction speed has been increased to its theoretical limits.
The damaged internal frame has also been adjusted.
Based on Mikazuki's melee combat abilities, the forearms have been enlarged and sharp claws made of rare metal are attached to the fingertips.
In addition, the Hashmal's tail parts transplanted to its back can be used as a Tail Blade to unleash a diverse array of thrusting attacks.
It also has a new main armament, an Extra Large Mace, which was developed to handle combat with enemy ships.

An improved form of the Gundam Barbatos.
This form is the result of the Barbatos being overhauled at Saisei's Mobile Suit workshop after it sustained too much damage from the fierce battle against the Graze Ein in Edmonton.
Its reaction speed and maneuverability have been increased based on the combat data of Mikazuki Augus, and the frame's movements have been more closely tied to the pilot's senses through the Alaya-Vijnana System.
Based on Mikazuki's preference for close-quarters combat, the exterior has been refitted with curved armor that makes it easier to deflect attacks, and specially designed thrusters have been mounted to the waist.
In addition, many new weapons have been developed for the suit, including a Sword Mace and Twin Maces for melee combat, as well as Arm 200mm Cannons incorporated into its armor.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by House Warren.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
This unit went missing after the war, until it was discovered at the bottom of a massive lunar crater by House Warren, who treated it as a family heirloom in the generations to follow.
When House Warren came under accusation of illicit business dealings and was destroyed, it found its way onto the black market.
By the time Tanto Tempo bought it, it had been stripped of its armaments and armor, leaving only the base frame.
In the time following that purchase, it was given a restoration of sorts by cobbling together various Mobile Suit parts, making it asymmetrical in appearance.
It maintains center-of-mass balance with chest-mounted boost armor.
Its weapons include a foldable large close-combat weapon called the Demolition Knife and a 210mm Anti-Material Rifle that is part of the standard loadout of House Warren machines.

A mobile suit that Suletta Mercury brings with her to school.
It is equipped with a shield made up of GUND-BITs, a next-generation remote-controlled swarm weapon system.

The Aerial has been renovated by the Shin Sei Development Corporation.
As well as repairs to the damage it suffered in the duel with Grassley House, it has received updates and additions to its external armor and equipment.

A prototype machine developed by the Ochs Earth Corporation and the Vanadis Institute.
Because it was designed with absolutely no guarantee of preserving the pilot's life, it lost its competition with the Lfrith, and all records of its research and development were subsequently deleted.
During the Vanadis Incident, it was confiscated by the Space Assembly League and secretly kept in storage.
